dinner.title: Sheet-pan chicken fajitas
steps: Slice the chicken, bell peppers, and onion.
Toss with the oil and all the spices.
Roast at 425°F for 20–25 minutes.
Serve in warm tortillas with sour cream and cheese.

lunch.title: Turkey & cheese sandwich

dinner.title: Ground beef spaghetti bolognese + garlic bread
ingredients: 1 jar marinara (or 28-oz crushed tomatoes + 1 tsp Italian seasoning)
steps: Brown the ground beef with the diced onion and garlic.
Add the marinara and simmer 20 minutes.
Toss with cooked spaghetti.
Top with parmesan and serve with garlic bread.

lunch.title: PB&J or tuna salad

dinner.title: Pork chops, roasted potatoes, green beans
steps: Season chops with salt, pepper, garlic powder, and paprika.
Sear 4 minutes per side, then rest 5 minutes.
Roast quartered potatoes (tossed in oil + rosemary) at 425°F for 30 minutes.
Steam or roast green beans alongside.

lunch.title: Leftover bolognese (Tue dinner)

dinner.title: Tilapia tacos with cabbage slaw + lime crema
steps: Season fillets with cumin, chili powder, and salt.
Pan-sear 3 minutes per side.
Slaw: shredded cabbage + juice of 1 lime + a pinch of salt.
Crema: sour cream + lime juice + zest.
Assemble in tortillas.

lunch.title: Turkey & cheese sandwich

dinner.title: Build-your-own bagel pizzas (movie night)
steps: Split the mini bagels.
Spread marinara and sprinkle mozzarella, then add toppings.
Bake at 400°F for 8–10 minutes until the cheese melts.
Serve with carrot sticks and ranch on the side.

lunch.title: Leftover pork chops + potatoes, chopped over greens (Wed dinner)

dinner.title: Backyard cookout — burgers, hot dogs, grilled chicken sausage
steps: Grill burgers (salt/pepper, cheese near the end), hot dogs, and chicken sausages together — stagger start times since cook times differ.
Sauté extra bell peppers and onion as a sausage topping.
Serve with buns, condiments, and chips.
Watermelon for dessert.

lunch.title: Leftover bagel pizza, reheated (Fri dinner)

dinner.title: Steak night — baked potatoes, Caesar salad
steps: Salt the steaks 40 minutes ahead.
Sear in screaming-hot cast iron 3–4 minutes per side with butter and garlic; rest 10 minutes.
Bake potatoes at 400°F for 60 minutes.
Caesar: romaine, dressing, croutons, parmesan.

lunch.title: Leftover cookout fixings — chopped burger over greens, or a cold hot dog/sausage sandwich (Sat dinner)

dinner.title: Jalapeño cheddar cornbread crust chicken pot pie
ingredients: Smoked paprika, mustard, red pepper flakes, turmeric, nutmeg
steps: Sauté carrots, onion, celery, and garlic in butter.
Build a creamy filling with flour, chicken broth, and half-and-half, seasoned with thyme, sage, rosemary, smoked paprika, mustard, red pepper flakes, turmeric, and nutmeg.
Stir in shredded chicken and peas.
Top with a one-bowl jalapeño cheddar cornbread batter (browning the butter first adds a nutty depth).
Bake until the crust is golden and set.
Full recipe: moribyan.com/jalapeno-cheddar-cornbread-crust-chicken-pot-pie

lunch.title: Leftover steak, sliced cold on a sandwich (Sun dinner, Week 1)

dinner.title: Chicken and rice soup — big batch, surgery day
steps: Sauté the onion, carrots, and celery.
Add broth, rice, and diced chicken.
Simmer 20 minutes until the rice is tender.
Season with salt, pepper, and a squeeze of lemon.
Makes a big pot — eat about half Tuesday and hold the rest for Wednesday, so there's no cooking the day after surgery.

lunch.title: Turkey & cheese sandwich (keep it light/simple — surgery day)

dinner.title: Leftover chicken and rice soup — recovery day 2
dinner.note: No cooking needed — reheat the second half of Tuesday's big pot of soup.

lunch.title: Cheese quesadilla + black beans — easy, soft recovery food

dinner.title: Baked lemon-garlic tilapia, rice, roasted zucchini
steps: Lay fillets in a baking dish, top with butter, minced garlic, lemon slices, salt, and pepper.
Bake at 400°F for 12–15 minutes.
Roast zucchini rounds alongside (oil, salt, 15 minutes).
Serve with rice.

lunch.title: Egg salad or tuna salad sandwich

dinner.title: Nachos (movie night)
steps: Spread tortilla chips on a sheet pan.
Scatter warmed black beans and shredded cheese over the top.
Bake at 400°F for 8–10 minutes until melted.
Top with salsa and sour cream.

lunch.title: Leftover baked tilapia, flaked over greens (Thu dinner)

dinner.title: Grilled cheddar jalapeño brats with peppers & onions, oven fries, corn on the cob
steps: Grill or pan-sear brats 12–15 minutes, turning occasionally, until browned and heated through.
Sauté sliced bell peppers + onion in butter until soft, 8–10 minutes.
Oven fries: cut potatoes into wedges, toss in oil + salt, 425°F for 35 minutes, flip once.
Boil or grill the corn.
Serve brats on buns topped with the peppers and onions, mustard on the side.

lunch.title: Turkey & cheese sandwich (nachos don't reheat well for lunch)

dinner.title: Steak fajita bowls (rice, peppers, corn)
steps: Slice the steak thin against the grain.
Sear hard, 2–3 minutes.
Sauté the peppers and onions.
Serve over rice with corn, cheese, and lime.

lunch.title: Leftover brat, sliced into a bun with the peppers and onions (Sat dinner)

dinner.title: Ground beef tacos (taco Tuesday, a day early)
steps: Brown 1 lb beef; add the chili powder, cumin, garlic powder, paprika, and ¼ cup water.
Simmer 5 minutes.
Serve in tortillas with lettuce, cheese, sour cream, and tomato.

lunch.title: Leftover steak fajita bowl fixings (Week 2 Sun dinner)

dinner.title: Grilled or pan-seared chicken Caesar wraps
steps: Season and sear chicken breasts 6–7 minutes per side; slice.
Toss chopped romaine with Caesar dressing, parmesan, and crushed croutons.
Roll everything in large tortillas.

lunch.title: Taco salad — leftover taco beef over greens (Mon dinner)

dinner.title: Blackened tilapia with cheese grits + sautéed spinach
steps: Coat fillets in blackening seasoning (paprika, cayenne, garlic powder, oregano, salt).
Sear in butter 3 minutes per side.
Grits: 1 cup grits in 4 cups water, 20 minutes, finish with cheddar + butter.
Sauté spinach with garlic 2 minutes.

lunch.title: Turkey & cheese sandwich

dinner.title: Pork chop fried rice (uses leftover rice + carrots)
steps: Dice the pork chops, sear, and remove.
Scramble 2 eggs and remove.
Stir-fry diced carrots + peas, add cold cooked rice, soy sauce, and sesame oil.
Fold everything back in with sliced green onion.

lunch.title: Leftover blackened tilapia + grits (Wed dinner)

dinner.title: DIY quesadillas (movie night)
steps: Layer shredded cheese (and any mix-ins) into a tortilla.
Fold and toast in a dry skillet 2–3 minutes per side until crisp and melted.

lunch.title: Leftover pork chop fried rice (Thu dinner)

dinner.title: Baked ziti with ground beef (makes leftovers)
steps: Brown 1 lb beef, add marinara, and simmer.
Toss with cooked ziti, dollops of ricotta, and a handful of spinach.
Top with mozzarella.
Bake at 375°F for 25 minutes.

lunch.title: Leftover quesadilla, reheated (Fri dinner)

dinner.title: Steak night
ingredients: A simple side — baked potato, rice, or orzo
steps: Salt the steaks 40 minutes ahead.
Sear in hot cast iron 3–4 minutes per side with butter and garlic; rest 10 minutes.
Serve with a simple side — baked potato, rice, or orzo, whatever's on hand.

lunch.title: Leftover baked ziti (Sat dinner) — carries into Monday of Week 4 too

dinner.title: Chicken burrito bowls (rice, beans, corn, salsa)
steps: Season and sear the chicken; slice.
Build bowls: rice, warmed black beans, corn, salsa, cheese, sour cream.
Squeeze of lime.

lunch.title: Leftover baked ziti (Week 3 Sat dinner)

dinner.title: Parmesan-crusted tilapia + orzo + roasted asparagus
steps: Mix ½ cup grated parmesan, ¼ cup breadcrumbs, and 1 tsp garlic powder.
Brush fillets with mayo or butter, then press on the crust.
Bake at 425°F for 12 minutes.
Roast asparagus alongside (10 minutes).
Serve with buttered orzo.

lunch.title: Leftover chicken burrito bowl fixings (Mon dinner)

dinner.title: Korean-style beef bowls over rice
steps: Brown 1 lb ground beef.
Sauce: ¼ cup soy sauce, 2 tbsp brown sugar, 3 cloves garlic, 1 tsp sesame oil, a pinch of red pepper flakes. Simmer 3 minutes.
Serve over rice with green onion; cucumber slices on the side.

lunch.title: Turkey & cheese sandwich

dinner.title: Steak + veggie kebabs (end-of-month cleanout night)
steps: Cube the steak; skewer or skillet-sear with chunks of whatever produce is left (peppers, onion, zucchini).
Season simply — salt, pepper, garlic.
Serve over the last of the rice or orzo.

lunch.title: Leftover Korean beef bowl (Wed dinner)

dinner.title: Mini sliders (movie night)
steps: Form small beef patties and press them flat.
Griddle 3 minutes per side, melting cheese on top.
Serve on mini buns with ketchup and pickles on the side.

lunch.title: Leftover steak kebabs (Thu dinner)
